monthly_review.summary: You've significantly increased intensity this month while reducing volume, with notable gains in pace and aerobic training effect — but cadence and efficiency show early signs of strain under higher HR zones.
monthly_review.highlight: Your average cadence jumped +23 spm this month (151 vs 128), coinciding with a 72 sec/km pace improvement — but stride length dropped to 68–79m (vs prior ~106m on 3km easy run), suggesting form compression at faster paces.

goal_tracker.message: You need 37.1 more km across 13 remaining sessions — averaging 2.9 km per session — to match last month’s volume. Given your recent 5.1–5.6 km runs, you can achieve this with just 7 more runs of ~5.3 km each.

insights.title: Efficiency improved despite higher HR
insights.body: Aerobic efficiency rose from 12.54 to 12.88 m/sec while avg HR increased 9 bpm — indicating better oxygen utilization per unit effort, likely due to recent Zone 3–4 work (36% and 14% of time this month).

insights.title: Zone 2 volume collapsed; Zone 3 dominates
insights.body: Zone 2 dropped from 45% to 46% (nearly stable), but Zone 3 surged to 36% (same % as last month) — yet total runs fell 79%, meaning nearly all runs this month were Zone 3+ (vs mixed Z1–Z3 last month). This compresses recovery opportunity.

insights.title: Cadence–stride tradeoff emerging
insights.body: Cadence rose to 151 spm (up from 128), but stride length collapsed to 68–79 cm on recent 5km runs — versus 106 cm on the 3km, 115 bpm easy run — suggesting compensatory shortening under fatigue or intensity.

insights.title: Aerobic TE sustained despite low volume
insights.body: Avg aerobic TE held at 2.9 (vs 2.7 last month) with only 3 runs — meaning each session delivered high physiological stimulus (e.g., 3.3 TE on 5.1km @ 154 bpm), increasing acute load without adequate recovery buffer.

recommendations.action: Do your next run as a strict 5km Zone 2 effort (HR ≤135 bpm), maintaining cadence ≥145 spm and stride length ≥85 cm (use real-time Garmin stride length display if available).
recommendations.reason: Your Zone 2 time dropped to just 1% this month (vs 8% last month), and your 3km easy run showed optimal form (106 cm stride, 133 bpm, 94 spm) — rebuilding Zone 2 volume is critical for recovery and aerobic base resilience.

recommendations.action: Add a 10-minute post-run cadence drill: 1 min @ 160 spm (metronome), 1 min easy, repeat 5x — immediately after your next two runs.
recommendations.reason: Cadence jumped +23 spm but stride shortened sharply (to 68 cm); this drill reinforces neuromuscular coordination without fatigue, helping decouple cadence from excessive stride reduction.

recommendations.action: Limit any run >4.5 km to HR ≤148 bpm for the next 7 days — use real-time HR alert on Garmin to pause and walk if exceeded.
recommendations.reason: Your 5.1km run at 154 bpm produced vertical oscillation of 8.3 cm (highest this month) and ground contact of 317 ms — both signs of accumulating fatigue; capping HR preserves form and reduces injury risk.

weekly_focus.description: Prioritize Zone 2 consistency, neuromuscular coordination, and objective form metrics (stride length, vertical oscillation) over pace or distance — re-establishing sustainable stimulus before progressing intensity.
suggested_sessions: 4km progressive run: first 2km Z2, last 2km Z3 (HR 140–148 bpm)
